original: Gayll is here, resting against her massive, gleaming hoverbike. She fiddles with a small, wrist-mounted computer to pass the time, sneaking glances at the station’s passersby.
Beyond the ticket kiosk is fare dodger detention.
It is empty at the moment, since you freed Hureya.

At the far end of the platform is a bizarre sight that takes you a few moments to get your head around. In a box demarcated in hazard yellow is some kind of metallic holding device, and bent over, head and paws trapped in it, is a big, naked hyrax. Her long, messy slice of blonde hair is vaguely familiar.

Shineglass Station is less a building and more a gathering place beneath the shade of a roof -- it’s a fifty-yard-long, five-wide concrete platform in the middle of the wasteland that runs parallel to a maglev rail that against all odds is still intact and humming with power. The west end of the station’s been hit by an explosive, collapsing the roof and shattering a few yards of the platform, but otherwise the place has survived the Glass War relatively intact. There’s even a still-functioning kiosk on the east end of the station, beside which is an electronic ticket terminal.\n\nFolk of various races are standing around the place: bird-like taurs, leafy-green reptilians, fluffy caniforms, and even a couple of wary humans.
